validator_outputs.command: test -f ios-from-docc.json && test -f ios-surface-comparison.md && python3 validator

notes: ABI source: AmitySDK.xcframework ios-arm64_x86_64-simulator .abi.json (already in vendor dir — no new SDK clone needed). Stats delta vs regex: types 380→393, protocols 27→37, members 2485→2761. Key findings: (1) ABI would have caught ~30/42 doc-as-test findings at extract time — all 26 SIGNATURE_CHANGE and 4 REMOVED_API. The 3-arg→2-arg observe() arity change is visible in ABI signature.parameters; the AmityChannelMembershipFilter .member/.muted rename is visible as name-absent. (2) Regex over-reaches into EkoStreamPublisher/HaishinKit streaming module (149 refs) — ABI correctly scopes to AmitySDK module only. (3) ABI surfaces Combine $-publishers (AmityClient.$sessionState etc.) and protocol members (AccessTokenHandler/Renewal) that regex missed. (4) 52 'regex-only, non-streaming' refs need per-case investigation before swapping ios.json — some are regex artefacts (same-name enum constructors), some may be real ABI gaps in nested type handling. Recommendation: proceed with swap after resolving those 52 refs. Doc-as-test layer should be retained (catches preamble/scaffold and type-inference issues that surface extraction cannot).
